question.text: Which of the following is TRUE about the performance track record disclosed in the SID?

answer.text: It must include comparison with the scheme's benchmark using Total Return Index (TRI)
It can show only absolute returns without any benchmark comparison
It is compared with the Price Return Index (PRI) of the benchmark
Performance track record is disclosed only in the Fact Sheet, not in the SID

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The SID must include the scheme's performance track record WITH benchmark comparison, and this benchmark must be the Total Return Index (TRI) as mandated by SEBI. This ensures investors can evaluate fund manager skill against a fair and complete benchmark.
**The Trap:** Performance cannot be shown without benchmark comparison — that would be misleading. PRI is the old standard and no longer acceptable. Performance IS part of the SID (as well as Fact Sheets) — saying it is only in Fact Sheets is incorrect.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM V-B and XII consistently test the TRI mandate and its rationale. The shift from PRI to TRI was a landmark regulatory change for investor protection.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When reviewing a scheme's performance in the SID, always check whether it consistently outperforms the TRI benchmark across multiple time periods (1-year, 3-year, 5-year). Outperformance in just one period may be luck; consistent outperformance suggests genuine skill.
